hook: 스테이블코인 규제가 은행권으로 확산되는 신호라 PM 관점에서 꼭 짚어볼 만합니다.
title: Big Banks Weigh Joint Stablecoin Under New Rules
passage: Several large banks are studying whether to launch a shared stablecoin, a digital token that is always worth one dollar. New rules now let banks issue such tokens if they hold safe assets to back every coin. Supporters say a joint coin would cut costs and speed up payments between companies. Critics warn that a few big banks could gain too much control over how money moves. Regulators want strict limits on reserves and clear reporting, so users can trust that each token can be redeemed at any time. The plan is still early, and no bank has committed real money yet. Analysts expect a pilot program later this year if the rules become final and the largest lenders agree on shared standards.

glossary.stablecoin: token pegged to a stable value

questions: According to the passage, what must banks hold in order to issue a stablecoin?
In this passage, what does the word "redeem" mean?
Do you think a stablecoin shared by several big banks is a good idea? Give 2-3 sentences with reasons.
modelAnswers: They must hold safe assets that back every coin they issue.
"Redeem" means to exchange the token back for cash at any time.

I think it could help because it may lower costs and make payments faster. However, I worry that only a few banks would control the system, which could reduce competition. Strong rules on reserves would be needed to keep it safe.

hook: 실물자산 토큰화가 국채로 넘어오는 흐름이라 토큰화에 관심 있으시면 유용합니다.
title: Tokenized Treasuries Pass Ten Billion in Value
passage: The total value of tokenized government bonds has passed ten billion dollars for the first time. Tokenization means putting a real-world asset, like a bond, onto a blockchain so it can be traded quickly and split into small pieces. Big asset managers now offer these products to give clients a safe place to earn steady yield. Because the tokens settle in minutes, firms can move cash in and out at any hour, even on weekends. Supporters believe this trend will bring more traditional money into crypto markets. Still, some experts caution that rules differ across countries, and a sudden change could freeze trading. For now, demand keeps rising as investors search for reliable returns without leaving the digital world.
questions: According to the passage, what does "tokenization" allow a real-world asset to do?
In this passage, what does the word "yield" mean?
Would you invest in tokenized government bonds? Explain your view in 2-3 sentences.
modelAnswers: It lets the asset be traded quickly and split into small pieces on a blockchain.
"Yield" means the income or return that money earns over time.
I might invest a small amount because the bonds seem safe and settle very fast. I like that I can move cash even on weekends. Still, I would first check how the rules apply in my country.

hook: OKX 등 거래소의 규제 대응 사례라 거래소 동향을 보시는 데 참고됩니다.
title: Exchange Adds Proof-of-Reserves to Rebuild Trust
passage: A major crypto exchange has expanded its proof-of-reserves system, which lets anyone check that the company holds enough assets to cover customer deposits. After several firms failed in past years, users now demand clear evidence that their money is safe. The exchange publishes a report each month and uses an outside auditor to confirm the numbers. Customers can also verify their own balance with a simple online tool. The company says transparency helps it meet stricter rules in many regions and win back trust. Rivals are expected to follow, since regulators increasingly treat reserve reports as a basic requirement. Experts note that proof-of-reserves shows assets but not debts, so it is a useful step rather than a complete guarantee of safety.

glossary.tool: a means to do a task

questions: According to the passage, what does a proof-of-reserves system let people check?
In this passage, what does the word "transparency" mean?
How important is proof-of-reserves when choosing an exchange? Answer in 2-3 sentences.
modelAnswers: It lets people check that the exchange holds enough assets to cover customer deposits.
"Transparency" means being open and clear so others can see the truth.

It is quite important to me because past failures showed that hidden problems can hurt users. A monthly report checked by an auditor makes me more confident. Still, I would remember that it does not show the company's debts.
